svg.text: Self-correcting DFIR loop on a typed read-only MCP boundary, with per-call audit anchoring every claim to a tool execution

g.text: For N iterations: build prompt -> spawn investigator subprocess -> run validator -> if not converged + within budget, build follow-up prompt with flagged claims, loop
Hard caps enforced before each iter
spawned as a subprocess; cannot read each other's state except via the shared audit log
No shell. No filesystem-by-path. No web access. No sub-agent fan-out.
38 typed read-only tools; Pydantic input schemas
Every call: validate args -> run subprocess (argv-list)
-> parse -> write audit row -> return parsed dict
query_rows · re-parse + filter any prior call's full row list by exec_id
subprocess invocation · argv-list only · shell=True never used
vol · fls / icat / mmls / fsstat · ewfinfo / ewfverify
tagged claims + exec_id citations · validator scores
PIDs, IPs, paths, ISO ts, hashes, inodes, emails
v5: prose / tag / prefix-lookup; paren-aware negation
every token must appear in parsed data (or be negated)
Haiku 4.5 reads parsed JSON; verdict in {V, X, R, ?}
If not converged: flagged claims -> next iteration's prompt
/cases/<CASE>/ — validated at every MCP call via validate_evidence_path() (allow-list resolver, symlink-safe)
agent has no way to modify /cases/
denied via --disallowed-tools; no Bash mcp tool
extract_exec_id chain only; no path arg
no http/curl/wget tools in MCP inventory

svg.text: SIFT-OWL · find-evil-hackathon · 38 tools (Phase 1 + 1.5 + 3) · 258 unit tests · validator v0..v5 · MIT
